title: Requesting beta testers GFOS PSP
wikitext:
[]: Requesting beta testers (GFOS PSP)             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat Nov 15, 2008 11:52 am Just after a few people who would like to trial the PSP conversion of GFOS I am currently doing. On the 11th those who would like to participate will receive it with instructions on how to install it onto psp. I believe this will run on any firmware level. The game will have a few problems such as sound, which I'm working on.

[]:             Quote Post by Gib » Sat Nov 15, 2008 12:56 pm Is the original PlayStation's GOS in english or what? I've a PSP so that I could trial it.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat Nov 15, 2008 1:13 pm Its the original GFOS for windows 95 with the English translation. If I am successful with this I would like to do it with the second one (GFOS2).

[]:             Quote Post by Gib » Sat Nov 15, 2008 1:26 pm Wow! I didn't know that it works with PSP. Are you using DosBox?

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat Nov 15, 2008 1:29 pm No its just a decompile and recompile

[]:             Quote Post by Gib » Sat Nov 15, 2008 1:33 pm And what problems are there? Sound, graphics...

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat Nov 15, 2008 1:43 pm Only sound at this time everything else i can do. There is no way to convert anything like this at this time with the sound. I'm working on a patch for the program although its now 0339am so I'm going to bed. Sound is no problem once I work it out because its all decompiled.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sat Nov 15, 2008 3:46 pm I am interested and confused. I kinda wish I had a PSP now just for this.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sat Nov 15, 2008 5:28 pm But you know, this game is just awful. I played it 1 or 2 years ago, and I was disapointed by so much mediocrity, especially in the dialogues. I think I should have sticked to my Japanese saturn version. This game's really better when you don't understand anything about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 15, 2008 7:30 pm I might be interested in testing this out--I'm not really sure yet. My PSP firmware is a custom 3.90 one btw.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat Nov 15, 2008 8:13 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: My PSP firmware is a custom 3.90 one btw.

[]:             Quote Post by Gib » Sun Nov 23, 2008 4:14 am Are there news about the project?

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sun Nov 23, 2008 4:16 am Ill have it up for testing on what i have done so far in 2 weeks when i get home finally.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Mon Dec 15, 2008 7:19 am Ok have a better Idea. I did manage to convert the game up to when mana comes. I just ran out of time. But I stumbled across this other little program. Does anybody have GFOS, Ayanimi Raising, Eva and Friend or any other Eva games that were released on Playstation X? If so I have found a quick and effective way to convert the "FULL" game across from cd to psp with all features working.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed Dec 17, 2008 9:07 am Thanks for the help thus far Gib. I just have to play with a few more settings then it should be right for the beta. Well here it is Girl Friend of Steel for PSP

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 17, 2008 12:43 pm That was cool being able to watch some of what you have so far. By the way, other Eva games for Playstation: GoS2, Ayanami with Asuka Raising Project, Secret of Evangelion, Eva2 and Detective Evangelion were all released for Playstation 2. (Although some were also released on the PC. Secret of Eva and Eva2 were later released on PSP.)
summary:
[]: Requesting beta testers (GFOS PSP)             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat Nov 15, 2008 11:52 am Just after a few people who would like to trial the PSP conversion of GFOS I am currently doing. On the 11th those who would like to participate will receive it with instructions on how to install it onto psp. I believe this will run on any firmware level. The game will have a few problems such as sound, which I'm working on.

[]:  &nb
